issues: The draft's short description incorrectly states the Sockeyes 'sweep Kelowna 3-2 in final game' — 3-2 was the score of the final game, not the series result; the series was swept five games to none (5-0).

The draft's long description says 'five games to none' which is correct for the series but the short description is misleading/wrong by saying 'sweep Kelowna 3-2 in final game' without clarifying context.
The draft omits the notable caption detail identifying Dean Rutledge (#20) and Rob Sumner as celebrating alongside captain Trevor Dickie with the BC Junior League trophy.

The byline is listed as 'Elliott Pap' but the OCR shows 'By ELLIOTT PAP' — the name casing is fine, but the headline 'The \'Eyes have it' is the article text/title, not a standalone headline label; this appears accurate per OCR.
The draft describes Kurtenbach as 'former Vancouver Canuck captain and coach' which matches the OCR — this is accurate.
The draft omits the photo caption detail about Craig Hodge being credited for the photo.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes win their first BCJHL championship in eight years, defeating Kelowna Packers 3-2 in Game 5 to complete a five-game sweep of the best-of-nine final.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Orland Kurtenbach's Richmond Sockeyes captured their first BC Junior Hockey League championship in eight years on March 25, 1987, defeating the Kelowna Packers 3-2 at Richmond Arena to sweep the best-of-nine final series five games to none. The Sockeyes ran up 13 consecutive wins across three BCJHL playoff series, going through the entire playoffs without a loss. Captain Trevor Dickie hoisted the BC Junior League trophy alongside teammate Dean Rutledge (#20) and Rob Sumner. Kurtenbach, a former Vancouver Canuck captain and coach, expressed pride in winning in his first year of junior coaching, with the team's sights next set on the Centennial Cup national championship.
review.notes: The short description in the draft was misleading — "sweep Kelowna 3-2" reads as a series result rather than a game score. Corrected to clarify 3-2 was the Game 5 score and the series was a five-game sweep. Also added photo caption details (Rutledge #20, Sumner) which are Sockeyes-relevant and present in the OCR. Note the OCR caption references "CRAIG HODGE" as the photographer credit.
